name: plain-worker
documentation:
<!-- This file is compiled from plain-worker/plain/worker/README.md. Do not edit this file directly. -->

# Worker

Process background jobs with a database-driven worker.

```python
from plain.worker import Job
from plain.mail import send_mail

# Create a new job class
class WelcomeUserJob(Job):
    def __init__(self, user):
        self.user = user

    def run(self):
        send_mail(
            subject="Welcome!",
            message=f"Hello from Plain, {self.user}",
            from_email="welcome@plainframework.com",
            recipient_list=[self.user.email],
        )


# Instantiate a job and send it to the worker
user = User.objects.get(pk=1)
WelcomeUserJob(user).run_in_worker()
```

The worker process is run separately using `plain worker run`.

documentation: Find the next datetime that matches the schedule after the given datetime.

classes.name: plain.worker.models.JobRequest

methods.name: plain.worker.models.JobRequest.convert_to_job
documentation:
JobRequests are the pending jobs that are waiting to be executed.
We immediately convert them to JobResults when they are picked up.
Keep all pending job requests in a single table.

classes.name: plain.worker.models.Job

methods.name: plain.worker.models.Job.convert_to_result
documentation: Convert this Job to a JobResult.

methods.name: plain.worker.models.Job.as_json
documentation: A JSON-compatible representation to make it easier to reference in Sentry or logging
All active jobs are stored in this table.

class_variables.name: plain.worker.models.JobResultStatuses.LOST
documentation: Either process lost, lost in transit, or otherwise never finished

classes.name: plain.worker.models.JobResult
documentation: All in-process and completed jobs are stored in this table.

functions.name: plain.worker.cli.clear_completed
documentation: Clear all completed job results in all queues.

functions.name: plain.worker.cli.purge_processing
documentation: Delete all running and pending jobs regardless of queue.

functions.name: plain.worker.cli.run_job
documentation: Run a job class directly (and not using a worker).

classes.name: plain.worker.jobs.ModelInstanceParameter
documentation:
A string representation of a model instance,
so we can convert a single parameter (model instance itself)
into a string that can be serialized and stored in the database.

classes.name: plain.worker.jobs.JobType
documentation:
Metaclass allows us to capture the original args/kwargs
used to instantiate the job, so we can store them in the database
when we schedule the job.

methods.name: plain.worker.jobs.Job._in_progress
documentation: Get all JobRequests and Jobs that are currently in progress, regardless of queue.

methods.name: plain.worker.jobs.Job.get_unique_key
documentation:
A unique key to prevent duplicate jobs from being queued.
Enabled by returning a non-empty string.

Note that this is not a "once and only once" guarantee, but rather
an "at least once" guarantee. Jobs should still be idempotent in case
multiple instances are queued in a race condition.

methods.name: plain.worker.jobs.Job.get_retry_delay
documentation:
Calcluate a delay in seconds before the next retry attempt.

On the first retry, attempt will be 1.
